title: GameStop Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2022 Results
text: GameStop reported a net loss of $313.1 million for fiscal year 2022, representing a significant operating challenge. The company's adjusted EBITDA was negative $192.7 million for fiscal year 2022. For fiscal year 2022, the loss was expected to be $1.31 per share, wider than the year-ago loss of $1.14 per share, while full-year revenue of $5.88 billion would decline 2.2% year over year. GameStop announced fourth-quarter earnings results in March 2023 that detailed how well the video game retailer did over the key holiday shopping season. Most investors were looking for sales to rise about 4% to $2.2 billion. GameStop's comments on 2022 were expected to be the main focus of Wall Street's attention, with most investors looking for sales to fall slightly in the new fiscal year as gains in e-commerce and in new initiatives like non-fungible tokens and blockchain offset some of the loss from the shrinking store base. CEO Matt Furlong said in the last quarter that GameStop spent 2021 and 2022 on repairing our decayed foundation, reestablishing a culture of operational intensity, and setting the right long-term priority.
